hero.title: Parral to Camden
hero.subtitle: Helia Cerpa-Pérez — Allende, exile, and thirty years of housing advocacy on a London council estate

media.alt: Aníbal Pinto main avenue in Parral, Chile — the small agricultural city in the Maule region where Helia Cerpa-Pérez was born in 1943
media.caption: Parral, Maule, Chile — birthplace of Pablo Neruda and of Helia Cerpa-Pérez. The town later housed a DINA intelligence headquarters

media.alt: Salvador Allende, President of Chile, photographed at the United Nations in New York between 1970 and 1973
media.caption: Salvador Allende at the United Nations, c. 1970–73. Photograph by Bernard Gotfryd, Library of Congress. Helia worked within the Allende government

media.alt: The bombing of La Moneda presidential palace during the Chilean military coup of 11 September 1973
media.caption: La Moneda under bombardment, 11 September 1973. Biblioteca del Congreso Nacional de Chile. Three of Helia's uncles later appeared on the Valech torture-victim lists

media.alt: Estadio Nacional — detainees in the stands, soldiers on the track, September 1973
media.caption: The national stadium as mass prison — the bowl from the track. Helia is listed as CERPA PEREZ HELIA DEL C among the 405 women prisoners registered by the Corporación

media.alt: Estadio Nacional — detainees behind the perimeter fence, soldiers on the field, September–November 1973
media.caption: At the wire: the same enclosure, the guard up close. The Corporación’s register again names CERPA PEREZ HELIA DEL C

media.alt: The Palacio de la Moneda, Santiago, rebuilt after the 1973 coup — a symbol of Chilean democratic restoration
media.caption: La Moneda rebuilt. The Italian embassy in Santiago sheltered 700 people after the coup; Piero De Masi bought mattresses with his own money

media.alt: Chile Solidarity Campaign national demonstration at Trafalgar Square, London, 16 September 1979
media.caption: Trafalgar Square, 16 September 1979. Photograph by Gillfoto. The Chile Solidarity Campaign had sixty local committees and thirty affiliated trade unions by 1976

media.alt: Cover of the Cartilla del Retornado Nº 3, November 1985, listing Chileans banned from re-entering their country — including Helia Cerpa Pérez on page 12
media.caption: Cartilla del Retornado Nº 3, November 1985 — 'List of Persons Banned from Entry.' Published by the Comité Pro-Retorno de Exiliados with the Vicaría de la Solidaridad. Helia appears on page 12

media.alt: Walker House, Ossulston Estate, Somers Town, Camden — the Grade II listed council estate where Helia settled after exile from Chile
media.caption: Walker House, Ossulston Estate, Somers Town. Built 1929–31 by the LCC. Helia represented the Ossulston Tenants and Residents Association for over a decade

media.alt: Portnall Park (Dormy House) on the Wentworth Estate, Surrey — the gated enclave where Pinochet was held at 28 Lindale Close, 1998–2000; photograph is not of Twin Pines or the picket
media.caption: Wentworth Estate, Virginia Water. Dormy House / Portnall Park (2008). Pinochet’s house was 28 Lindale Close — not this building. Helia joined the 503-day piquete at the estate gates; no suitable CC-licensed gate photo is in the repo

media.alt: Museo de la Memoria y los Derechos Humanos, Santiago — the national museum commemorating victims of the Chilean dictatorship
media.caption: Museo de la Memoria y los Derechos Humanos, Santiago, 2019. Photograph by Carlos Figueroa Rojas. The Valech commissions recognised over 40,000 victims
